focusPoints.label: Late nights — temporary push or default?
focusPoints.reason: Notes say Lin has worked late for about three weeks; one possibility is a short sprint to finish launches, but it’s worth clarifying whether this is becoming the new baseline pace.

focusPoints.label: What’s driving current load now that launches are done.
focusPoints.reason: Whether the next two weeks’ work still matches the earlier “ahead of schedule” push, or whether new priorities are quietly replacing it.

focusPoints.label: What only Lin should own vs delegate.
focusPoints.reason: Whether the work that requires Lin’s judgment is staying concentrated there, or whether late nights are masking a distribution/autonomy gap that could be fixed by delegating real ownership.

focusPoints.label: What Lin would want more of from you.
focusPoints.reason: What they need from you that they aren’t currently getting—especially around air cover, decision-making, or removing bottlenecks so the pace can normalize after the launches.

name: Lin
notes: Lin has delivered two major features ahead of schedule but has worked late for about three weeks. I need to understand whether this pace was a temporary push or is becoming a default. Because Lin tends to brush off concern, I should ask directly what is driving the load now that those launches are done. I also want clarity on what work only Lin should own versus what could be delegated, since sustained late nights at this level can hide a distribution or autonomy problem.
